extra.message: User-controlled 'name' query parameter flows into a Jinja2 Template().render() call via f-string interpolation. The name parameter from the URL query string (line 19) is concatenated into an f-string on line 38, then the resulting string is passed to Jinja2's Template constructor and rendered on line 41. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI), which in Jinja2 can lead to remote code execution (e.g., {{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}).

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'name' query parameter is interpolated directly into an HTML f-string without any sanitization or escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript (e.g., ?name=<script>alert(1)</script>) which will be rendered in the browser, leading to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Hardcoded secret token '5u93R53Cr3tT0k3n' in source code (line 15). The FIXME comment on line 15 indicates this value should be loaded from an environment variable via os.getenv('SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN'). Hardcoding secrets in source code exposes them to anyone with access to the repository.

DEBUG=True is set in the environment template file .env_temp, which is loaded by config.py (line 7) and passed to FastAPI as debug=True in app/main.py (line 14). Enabling debug mode in production exposes detailed error tracebacks, internal application state, and potentially sensitive configuration data to end users.
